pr.title: add stage.c function declarations to the header file
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Added 43 function declarations from stage.c to src/melee/gr/stage.h, mostly stage camera, blast-zone, pause-camera, and fixed-camera accessors/helpers. The PR was a small header-only integration change with no review comments in the available slice.
what_changed: Extended src/melee/gr/stage.h after the StageInfo typedef with prototypes for stage.c functions.
Declared camera bounds getters: get_cam_bounds_left_offset, get_cam_bounds_right_offset, get_cam_bounds_top_offset, and get_cam_bounds_bottom_offset.

Declared stage camera parameter getters including get_cam_pan_angle_radians, get_cam_max_depth, get_cam_zoom_rate, get_cam_fixed_zoom, get_cam_track_ratio, get_cam_track_smooth, get_cam_fixed_fov, and directional camera angle getters.
Declared blast-zone offset getters for right, left, top, and bottom.
Declared pause-camera z-position getters: get_pausecam_zpos_min, get_pausecam_zpos_init, and get_pausecam_zpos_max.

Declared several still-unknown stage/camera helpers using address-suffixed names such as calc_unk_cam_y_80224B98, calc_unk_cam_y_bounds_80224BC4, unk_set_vec3_to_cam_offset_80224BF8, and func_80224CAC through func_8022532C.
smart_moves: Centralized stage.c declarations in stage.h so other translation units could include the known prototypes instead of relying on implicit declarations or duplicating externs.

Used meaningful descriptive names for many camera and blast-zone accessors where behavior was apparently understood, while preserving address-based func_ names for functions whose purpose was not yet clear.
Kept uncertain names visibly marked with unk or raw function-address suffixes, avoiding overclaiming semantics before decomp evidence was stronger.
Included argument and return types in the declarations, exposing useful type information such as Vec pointers, f32 camera values, BOOL results, and s32 indices.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a module with many cross-file call sites, moving discovered prototypes into the module header is useful infrastructure even if no function bodies change.
Accessor-like stage/camera functions can often be named by the field or camera property they return, while unknown helpers should keep address-linked names until behavior is proven.
Header declarations can encode incremental reverse-engineering knowledge: parameter types, return types, and partial naming all help later matching and cleanup.
A small header-only PR can still materially reduce friction for future decomp work by making stage APIs available to other files.
naming_conventions: Camera accessor names use get_cam_* for camera bounds, pan angle, zoom, track, depth, fixed camera, and angle values.
Blast-zone accessors use get_blast_zone_*_offset with directional suffixes right, left, top, and bottom.
Pause-camera accessors use get_pausecam_zpos_* for min, init, and max z-position values.
Uncertain functions retain func_802xxxxx names or include an address suffix, for example calc_unk_cam_y_80224B98 and unk_set_vec3_to_cam_offset_80224BF8.
Vector parameters are typed as Vec* and sometimes named generically, e.g. arg0 or arg_vec, when exact semantics are not yet known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly diff or matching-specific discussion is present in the PR slice.
The practical matching tactic evidenced here is prototype consolidation: declaring return and parameter types in stage.h to support cleaner cross-file compilation and future decompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the available PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80224CAC through func_8022532C or replace the unknown stage camera helper names.
Search for later edits to src/melee/gr/stage.h to see whether these prototypes gained void parameter lists or more descriptive argument names.
Search stage.c and call sites for get_cam_info_x20 and get_cam_info_x24 to identify the underlying StageInfo fields.
Look for later stage camera or blast-zone struct work that replaces offset accessors with named struct fields.
